message:
type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom filters support: prompt decorator (direct + parameterized @prompt(filters={...}) usage), Prompt.from_str/from_file gain filters param, new create_jinja_env() helper centralizing jinja2.Environment creation + filter registration, _template_from_str/_template_from_file refactored to use it

type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding global filter registry: new module-level dict _FILTERS (or similar), register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) functions. Need to modify Prompt._template_from_str and Prompt._template_from_file to merge global registry filters into env.filters (local/explicit filters take precedence). Will touch the two _template_from_* classmethods and add new top-level functions near get_fn_name etc.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/prompts.py
region: create_jinja_env() helper + filter registry + _template_from_str/_template_from_file
code: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}


def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a global custom Jinja2 filter available to all prompt templates."""
    _FILTERS[name] = func


def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
    """Unregister a global custom Jinja2 filter."""
    _FILTERS.pop(name, None)


def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create a Jinja2 environment with the default filters and any custom filters.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
    filters
        A dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables. These take
        precedence over globally registered filters of the same name.

    Returns
    -------
    A configured `jinja2.Environment` instance.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args

    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters is not None:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env


@classmethod
def _template_from_str(_, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Template:
    # Dedent, and remove extra linebreak
    cleaned_template = inspect.cleandoc(content)

    # Add linebreak if there were any extra linebreaks that
    # `cleandoc` would have removed
    ends_with_linebreak = content.replace(" ", "").endswith("\n\n")
    if ends_with_linebreak:
        cleaned_template += "\n"

    # Remove extra whitespaces, except those that immediately follow a newline symbol.
    # This is necessary to avoid introducing whitespaces after backslash `\` characters
    # used to continue to the next line without linebreak.
    cleaned_template = re.sub(r"(?![\r\n])(\b\s+)", " ", cleaned_template)

    env = create_jinja_env(None, filters)

    return env.from_string(cleaned_template)

@classmethod
def _template_from_file(_, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Template:
    file_directory = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
    env = create_jinja_env(jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters)
    return env.get_template(os.path.basename(path))
note: create_jinja_env centralizes env creation; merges global _FILTERS registry then explicit local filters (local wins). from_str/from_file/prompt decorator will pass their filters= arg through; register_filter/unregister_filter manage the global registry.

region: create_jinja_env() helper
code: def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create a Jinja2 environment with the standard Outlines filters.

    Registered global filters (see `register_filter`) are included automatically.
    Locally-provided `filters` take precedence over both the built-in and the
    globally registered filters.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env
note: New helper centralizing env creation. _FILTERS is my global registry dict (module-level, populated by register_filter/unregister_filter). Proposing this signature so both from_str/from_file(filters=...) and the global registry compose: local filters win over global ones.

def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:

def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None

    ----------
    loader

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_str(_, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Template:
        # Dedent, and remove extra linebreak

        cleaned_template = re.sub(r"(?![\r\n])(\b\s+)", " ", cleaned_template)

        env = create_jinja_env(None, filters)

        return env.from_string(cleaned_template)

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_file(_, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Template:
        file_directory = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
        env = create_jinja_env(jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters)
        return env.get_template(os.path.basename(path))
